# Source Card: Clinical Decision Support Software: Guidance for Industry and Food and Drug Administration Staff

## Summary
This FDA final guidance (issued January 6, 2026; re-issued January 29, 2026; docket FDA-2017-D-6569) sets out FDA's current interpretation of the four criteria in section 520(o)(1)(E) of the FD&C Act that exclude certain clinical decision support software from the definition of a device. For the pediatric anemia CDS module it is the governing regulatory frame: it establishes that a rule engine is "Non-Device CDS" only if it displays/analyzes medical information, provides non-specific recommendations, and enables a health care professional to independently review the basis for those recommendations without relying primarily on them. It contains no clinical or laboratory thresholds — it constrains how CDS outputs may be framed, not what any lab cutoff is.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) All four 520(o)(1)(E) criteria must be met for non-device CDS status — locator: Section II, pp. 5-6 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:a2e3d96d8b5c9f4bd3ab6cd659617403ae79424d159a7632d866ccc3c355a887]"
- (ev_002) Criterion 4 requires enabling HCP independent review of the basis, not primary reliance — locator: Section II / Section IV(4), p. 6, 14-15 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:cac14dada569c5af17b0a0f2774ccebd42e4065878011689b111fee5caf65b10]"
- (ev_003) A specific preventive/diagnostic/treatment output or directive fails Criterion 3 — locator: Section IV(3), pp. 10-11 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:98f5af134c822d5761996b2b2f7b80cefb4b48b809b70958835c2437864fa498]"
- (ev_004) Intended use must be stated; time-critical/time-sensitive tasks do not meet Criterion 4 — locator: Section IV(4)(a), p. 14 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:4516e577f7ecda822287b1c837c24bac5755b2f4f73553a2707afb32b2248699]"
- (ev_005) Level of automation and time-critical nature (automation bias) are weighed for independent review — locator: Section IV(4), p. 15 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:a5d572e928e9bd2a7b84c4d4f507977a126fc21e5e255e92aa84b3af03460c69]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population/assay: not applicable — this is a device-classification regulatory guidance, not a clinical reference; it carries no age bands, sexes, gestational/postnatal ages, analyzers, or method dependence.
- Lifecycle: Final guidance, issued 2026-01-06 and re-issued 2026-01-29 (docket FDA-2017-D-6569); supersedes the September 28, 2022 CDS final guidance. Nonbinding ("Contains Nonbinding Recommendations"). The statutory criteria themselves are unchanged; the 2026 update revised FDA's interpretation (notably moving the time-critical consideration out of Criterion 3 and into Criterion 4).

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- Indirect: the guidance's Criterion 3/Criterion 4 boundary is the regulatory line the CDS converter must respect — the pediatric anemia module must present non-specific recommendations with a reviewable basis and must not issue specific diagnostic/treatment directives or serve time-critical decisions, or it risks meeting the device definition. It defines a design/scope boundary for the rule outputs, not a clinical red-flag or referral trigger.

## Limitations & conflicts
- No numeric threshold is present or locatable in this source (regulatory/definitional guidance only); therefore no pediatric threshold value is asserted from FDA2026_CDS — this is expected, not a fabrication gap.
- Guidance is nonbinding; it reflects FDA's current thinking and may be revised. Quotes are drawn from the guidance document PDF (fda.gov/media/109618/download); the KB `url` points to the FDA landing page for that document.
- No conflicts with other anemia-KB sources recorded (this source governs regulatory framing, not clinical values, so it does not compete with lab-threshold sources).
